test.note: Exit code 1 due to 3 pre-existing pm2-clj parity test failures (documented in AGENTS.md as known pre-existing issues unrelated to threat-radar). All 6 mission-related tests pass. Package-specific: radar-core 13/13 pass, signal-atproto 6/6 pass.

All 7 mission packages typecheck cleanly: radar-core, signal-atproto, signal-embed-browser, mcp-foundation, thread-assessment, threat-radar-mcp, threat-radar-web.

lint.note: Lint not yet configured for this workspace.

appliedUpdates.description: Documented Express 5 ParamsDictionary string|string[] type quirk with fix pattern
Documented standard tsconfig template for new packages and the 11 broken tsconfig fixes
Documented @promethean-os/embedding naming convention exception from @workspace/* pattern

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update or remove the 'CRITICAL: radar-core Type Restoration' section in both mission AGENTS.md and .factory/library/architecture.md — the types have been restored (commit a9bdfe1). Leaving stale 'MISSING' warnings causes future workers to attempt redundant work.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature restore-radar-core-types successfully restored all 6 schemas (SignalEvent, SignalEventProvenance, Thread, ThreadMembership, ConnectionOpportunity, ActionCard) to src/schema.ts. Build and typecheck pass.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add explicit error handling pattern to coding conventions: 'Use catch (err: unknown) with instanceof Error narrowing. Never use catch (err: any).' Example: catch (err: unknown) { const msg = err instanceof Error ? err.message : 'Unknown error'; }
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature fix-mcp-type-error committed code with catch (err: any) in two route handlers (main.ts lines 367, 378), violating the 'No any types' convention. The convention exists but lacks an explicit catch-block pattern example.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add exception to backend-worker skill's TDD step: 'For configuration-only tasks (workspace registration, tsconfig fixes) and type-only fixes (no runtime behavior change), tsc --noEmit is sufficient verification. TDD may be skipped with justification in handoff.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Three features (register-pnpm-workspace, fix-mcp-type-error, restore-radar-core-types) had no runtime behavior to test. All workers correctly deviated from TDD but noted it in skillFeedback. This is a consistent pattern worth codifying.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Consider updating the 'test' command from 'npx vitest run --reporter=verbose' (root-level only) to 'pnpm -r run test' (runs all package test scripts). Individual packages now have their own vitest configs and test scripts. Alternatively, document that per-package tests exist alongside the root command.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: packages/radar-core and packages/signal-atproto both have 'test': 'vitest run' in package.json. The root vitest.config.ts only picks up tests/*, not package-level tests.

rejectedObservations.observation: detect-secrets pre-commit hook flags test files with sha256: prefixed strings as secrets
rejectedObservations.reason: already-documented — this is already in AGENTS.md under 'Known Pre-Existing Issues' with the SKIP_PRECOMMIT_SECRETS=1 workaround

rejectedObservations.observation: dist/*.js files contain runtime Zod definitions suitable for copy-back to src when source is lost
rejectedObservations.reason: too-niche — this recovery pattern is specific to the one-time type restoration and unlikely to recur. Not worth documenting as general guidance.
